symptoms.symptom_name: Symptom onset immediately after reading abnormal thyroid results online

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tight temporal link to a frightening psychological trigger is characteristic of an acute panic reaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations with sensation of skipped/extra beats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpitations are a common somatic feature of panic; ectopic-beat awareness is heightened by anxiety.
A sensation of skipped/extra beats keeps arrhythmia on the differential and mandates an ECG that telehealth cannot provide.
Hyperventilation with a subjective inability to catch a full breath is a hallmark of panic.
Acute dyspnoea keeps PE in mind, though absence of pleuritic pain / DVT risk and normal saturation argue against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised weakness with sense of impending doom

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sense of impending doom is a core panic feature; generalised weakness reflects the autonomic surge and hyperventilation.
Absence of chest pain lowers the probability of an acute coronary event, though it does not exclude it.
Absence of syncope lowers the probability of a haemodynamically significant arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Longstanding mild unrefreshing sleep and subtle weight change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific chronic features that may loosely accompany early thyroid dysfunction but do not explain the acute presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Raised TSH with normal T4 and elevated TPO antibodies (recent screening bloods)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests and anti-TPO antibodies
investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated TSH, normal free T4, positive anti-thyroid peroxidase antibodies — subclinical autoimmune (Hashimoto's) thyroiditis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised TSH with normal T4 defines subclinical hypothyroidism; positive TPO antibodies point to autoimmune (Hashimoto's) aetiology.
A HIGH TSH is the opposite of the suppressed TSH seen in thyrotoxicosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sinus tachycardia HR 102, regular rhythm on auscultation, no dropped beats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A regular sinus tachycardia correlating with acute panic supports anxiety — but this is a sealed clinician auscultation finding; the AI Doctor cannot obtain it and must infer from history and arrange an ECG.
Regular rhythm without dropped beats argues against an irregular arrhythmia — but only the in-person clinician has this information.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 100% on room air, chest clear to auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal oxygen saturation and clear lungs argue against PE and hypoxia — a sealed clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thyroid gland not palpably enlarged or tender; normal reflexes without delayed relaxation phase

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of goitre and of a delayed reflex relaxation phase argues against overt hypothyroidism — a sealed clinician examination finding.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute panic constellation with cyberchondria trigger
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden multi-system somatic symptoms time-locked to a frightening health-information trigger, with negative cardiac red flags, is characteristic of an acute panic reaction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Palpitations with skipped-beat sensation warrant an ECG to exclude an arrhythmia; this cannot be done via chat and is the reason for prompt in-person review.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4) and anti-TPO antibodies in 6-8 weeks
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Subclinical hypothyroidism is monitored with repeat TFTs to track trajectory rather than treated acutely.

There is no clinical indication for emergency cardiac or PE imaging given negative red flags and normal sealed saturation; ordering these would be over-investigation driven by the patient's health anxiety.
